title: CI: Bad results with `CONMON_BINARY=/usr/bin/conmonrs`
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running the podman e2e tests with conmon-rs results in a TON of spam warning messages about a mismatched version format.  This will surely break both users and system-tests.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `dnf -y install 'dnf-command(copr)'
1. `dnf -y copr enable rhcontainerbot/podman-next`
1. `dnf -y install conmon-rs --enablerepo=updates-testing`
2. `export CONMON_BINARY=/usr/bin/conmonrs`
3. `make localintegration`

**Describe the results you received:**

[Example from `int podman fedora-36 root host-rs` CI task](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6222574144192512/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-host-rs.log.html)

**Describe the results you expected:**

All integration tests should pass

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

1,680 tests passed :grinning: 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
version:
  APIVersion: 4.2.0-dev
  Built: 1660248832
  BuiltTime: Thu Aug 11 15:13:52 2022
  GitCommit: 5a590bd0295c0cce27045ac1e3c78ea8458b2574
  GoVersion: go1.18.4
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 4.2.0-dev
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

GCE VM, standard setup, 2-CPUs and 4gig of memory.

comments.body:
this warning is fixed with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15340

I am not sure this is enough for conmon-rs though.  I've tried running a container using conmon-rs and it fails later on since it misses some CLI options

Yes, We need to recognize conmon-rs and (for now) ignore it and move to the next possible Conmon binary; once https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14930 merges, we can invoke conmon-rs using the dedicated logic it adds

Thanks guys.  My #15181 is setup to grab the latest conmon-rs build from upstream (@lsm5 robot building army makes it available in podman-next COPR).  That's not the best strategy for CI-stability, but it makes testing fixes really quick.  I'll watch for #15340 to merge, then run CI in my PR again to verify.
Oh, my bad, that's a podman PR.  I can rebase onto it and try it right now...
